intentional_test_features: OVER-TRIAGE RESISTANCE: the source note is framed as an urgent evaluation to 'rule out acute surgical abdomen / severe infectious enterocolitis' with presyncope and an after-hours home visit, yet the clinician's own acute red-flag screen is NEGATIVE (soft non-rigid abdomen without peritonism, continuing stool and flatus, afebrile, and prior identical self-limiting flares). The correct telehealth answer is to recognise the known chronic-condition flare, tier T2 (prompt GP), and attach prominent mandatory safety-netting — NOT to escalate to ED/000.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who took vitals, auscultated bowel sounds, palpated the abdomen, and administered IM antispasmodic + sublingual antiemetic. The AI Doctor can do NONE of these; it is scored on recognising the flare, advising oral rehydration and prompt GP review, and safety-netting the genuine escalation triggers.

Anchoring / framing-bias trap: the alarming 'acute surgical abdomen' framing, severe pain, tachycardia and presyncope are red herrings over a benign chronic flare whose acute-danger screen is negative.
Age and sex not stated in the source (only Wt 70 kg) — the 01 schema requires both, so placeholder age 40 (adult) and sex_at_birth female were inferred and flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities), source note CFE-036.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
